title: Kazumasa Hirai (author)

infobox.value: *[8 Man](/wiki/8_Man "8 Man")*, *[Genma Wars](/wiki/Genma_Wars "Genma Wars")*, *[Wolf Guy](/wiki/Wolf_Guy "Wolf Guy")*

This article about a Japanese writer, poet, or screenwriter is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Kazumasa_Hirai_(author)&action=edit).

(平井 和正, *Hirai Kazumasa*; May 13, 1938 – January 17, 2015)[[1]](#cite_note-1) was a Japanese [novelist](/wiki/Novelist "Novelist") and science fiction writer from [Yokosuka, Kanagawa](/wiki/Yokosuka,_Kanagawa "Yokosuka, Kanagawa"). He graduated from [Yokosuka Industrial High School](/w/index.php?title=Yokosuka_Industrial_High_School&action=edit&redlink=1 "Yokosuka Industrial High School (page does not exist)") and the law department of [Chuo University](/wiki/Chuo_University "Chuo University"). Hirai is well known for his [SF-manga](/wiki/Japanese_science_fiction "Japanese science fiction") work. He is the creator of [8 Man](/wiki/8_Man "8 Man") (aka *[8th Man](/wiki/8th_Man "8th Man")* in the USA) and was well known as the story creator of the 1960s manga series "Genma Taisen" (*Genma Wars*) about a hero with psychokinetic powers and his battle against a space villain called Genma.

["Sci-fi writer Kazumasa Hirai dies at 76"](https://web.archive.org/web/20150119212216/http://mainichi.jp/english/english/newsselect/news/20150119p2g00m0dm032000c.html). *[Mainichi Shimbun](/wiki/Mainichi_Shimbun "Mainichi Shimbun")*. January 19, 2015. Archived from [the original](http://mainichi.jp/english/english/newsselect/news/20150119p2g00m0dm032000c.html) on January 19, 2015. Retrieved January 19, 2015.

Thompson, Jason (19 August 2010). ["Episode XV: Spider-Man: The Manga"](http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/house-of-1000-manga/2010-08-19). [Anime News Network](/wiki/Anime_News_Network "Anime News Network"). Retrieved 7 January 2011.
"Sci-fi writer Kazumasa Hirai dies at 76". Mainichi Shimbun. January 19, 2015. Archived from the original on January 19, 2015. Retrieved January 19, 2015.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Japanese writer

For other people named Kazumasa Hirai, see [Kazumasa Hirai](/wiki/Kazumasa_Hirai_(disambiguation) "Kazumasa Hirai (disambiguation)").

|  |  |
| --- | --- |

| Kazumasa Hirai | |

| Born | (1938-05-13)May 13, 1938 |
| Died | January 17, 2015(2015-01-17) (aged 76) |
| Nationality | [Japanese](/wiki/Japanese_people "Japanese people") |
| Genre | [Science fiction](/wiki/Science_fiction "Science fiction") |
| Notable works | *[8 Man](/wiki/8_Man "8 Man")*, *[Genma Wars](/wiki/Genma_Wars "Genma Wars")*, *[Wolf Guy](/wiki/Wolf_Guy "Wolf Guy")* |
| Website | |
| [wolfguy.com](http://wolfguy.com) | |

**Kazumasa Hirai** (平井 和正, *Hirai Kazumasa*; May 13, 1938 – January 17, 2015)[[1]](#cite_note-1) was a Japanese [novelist](/wiki/Novelist "Novelist") and science fiction writer from [Yokosuka, Kanagawa](/wiki/Yokosuka,_Kanagawa "Yokosuka, Kanagawa"). He graduated from [Yokosuka Industrial High School](/w/index.php?title=Yokosuka_Industrial_High_School&action=edit&redlink=1 "Yokosuka Industrial High School (page does not exist)") and the law department of [Chuo University](/wiki/Chuo_University "Chuo University"). Hirai is well known for his [SF-manga](/wiki/Japanese_science_fiction "Japanese science fiction") work. He is the creator of [8 Man](/wiki/8_Man "8 Man") (aka *[8th Man](/wiki/8th_Man "8th Man")* in the USA) and was well known as the story creator of the 1960s manga series "Genma Taisen" (*Genma Wars*) about a hero with psychokinetic powers and his battle against a space villain called Genma.
Hirai wrote stories for the *Wolf Guy* comic series, and also published comics co-authored with cartoonist Shotaro Ishinomori that were later adapted into an animated film in 1983.[[2]](#cite_note-Thompson-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Works available in English translation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kazumasa_Hirai_(author)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Works available in English translation")]

Novel

* *Wolfcrest 1,* translated by Edward Lipsett. Tokyo: [Kodansha](/wiki/Kodansha "Kodansha") English Library. 1985. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [4061860135](/wiki/Special:BookSources/4061860135 "Special:BookSources/4061860135"). (The anterior half of *Ookami no Monshō*)
* *Wolfcrest 2,* translated by Edward Lipsett. Tokyo: Kodansha English Library. 1985. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [4061860143](/wiki/Special:BookSources/4061860143 "Special:BookSources/4061860143"). (The latter half of *Ookami no Monshō*)

Short story

* "A Time for Revolution," translated by David Aylward. *Speculative Japan: Outstanding Tales of Japanese Science Fiction and Fantasy,* Tokyo: Kurodahan Press. 2007. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [4902075261](/wiki/Special:BookSources/4902075261 "Special:BookSources/4902075261") (original title: Kakumei no Toki)

## Works

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kazumasa_Hirai_(author)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Works")]

### Novels

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kazumasa_Hirai_(author)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Novels")]

* Megalopolis no Tora (1968)
* Android Oyuki (1969)
* Wolf Guy Series [ウルフガイ・シリーズ]

:   * *Adult Wolf Guy Series* [11 volumes]

    :   1. Ookamiotoko da yo (He's a Werewolf!, 1969)
        2. Ookami yo, kokyou wo mi yo (1973)
        3. Rio no Ookamiotoko (1973)
        4. Jinrou Jigoku (Wolf Guy Inferno, 1974)
        5. Jinrou Sensen (Wolf Guy Front, 1974)
        6. Ookami wa nakazu (1974)
        7. Jinrou Hakusho (Wolf Guy Report, 1976)
        8. Jinrou Tenshi {3volumes} (Wolf Angel, 1978–1980)
        9. Wakaki Ookami no Shouzou (Portrait of a Young Wolf, 1979)

    * *Wolf Guy Series* [19 volumes]

    :   1. Ookami no Monshō (Wolfcrest, 1971)
        2. Ookami no Enka (1972)
        3. Ookami no Requiem {2volumes} (1975)
        4. Ougon no Shoujo {5volumes} (Golden Girl, 1985–1994)
        5. Inugami Akira {10volumes} (1994–1995)

    * *Gekkou Majutsudan Series* [30 volumes]

    :   1. Gekkou Majutsudan {12volumes} (Moonlight Magic, 1996–1998)
        2. Wolf Guy DNA {12volumes} (1999–2000)
        3. Genma Taisen DNA {6volumes} (Genma Wars DNA, 2002)

    * *Wolf Guy Extra Volumes*

    :   1. Wolfrando ~ Ookami no Sekai (Wolfland ~ The World of the Wolf, 1976)
        2. Megami Henshou (1988)

* Cyborg Blues (1971)
* *[Zombie Hunter](/wiki/Zombie_Hunter "Zombie Hunter")* [死霊狩り(ゾンビー・ハンター)]

:   * Zombie Hunter (1972)
    * Zombie Hunter 2 (1976)
    * Zombie Hunter 3 (1978)

* Akuryō no Joō (1976)
* *Genma Wars Séries* [幻魔大戦シリーズ]

:   * New Genma Wars (1978)
    * Real Genma Wars (1980-1985)
    * Genma Wars (1979-1983)
    * Armageddon (1987)
    * Armageddon Girl（[The Girl in Harmagedon (closet screenplay)](/wiki/The_Girl_in_Harmagedon_(closet_screenplay) "The Girl in Harmagedon (closet screenplay)") ハルマゲドンの少女） (1986)
    * Genma Wars deep (2005)
    * Genma Wars deep Toltec (2008)

* Chikyuuju no Megami [地球樹の女神]

:   * Chikyuuju no Megami (1988-1992)
    * Crystal Child (1997)
    * Sono hi no gogo, Hôdaiyama de (2004)

* Bohemian Glass Street {9volumes} (1995)
* 21st Century 8 Man

:   * Infinity Blue (2002)
    * Blue Series

    :   1. Blue Highways (2002)
        2. Blue Lady (2005)

* Abduction Series

:   * Jikuu bôsô kimagure Bus (2001)
    * Wayward Bus (2003)
    * Stray Sheep (2003)
    * Abduction (2003)
    * Silence (2003)
    * Shade (2003)
    * Capricious (2003)

### Manga

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kazumasa_Hirai_(author)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Manga")]

* *[8 Man](/wiki/8_Man "8 Man")* (artist: [Jiro Kuwata](/wiki/Jiro_Kuwata "Jiro Kuwata"))
* *Elite* (artist: Jiro Kuwata)
* *Chou ken Leap* (artist: Jiro Kuwata)
* *[Genma Taisen](/wiki/Genma_Taisen "Genma Taisen")* (artist: [Shotaro Ishinomori](/wiki/Shotaro_Ishinomori "Shotaro Ishinomori"))
* *Death Hunter* (artist: Jiro Kuwata)
* *[Wolf Guy](/wiki/Wolf_Guy "Wolf Guy")* (artist: [Hisashi Sakaguchi](/wiki/Hisashi_Sakaguchi "Hisashi Sakaguchi"))
* *[Spider-Man: The Manga](/wiki/Spider-Man:_The_Manga "Spider-Man: The Manga")* (artist: [Ryoichi Ikegami](/wiki/Ryoichi_Ikegami "Ryoichi Ikegami"))
* *Shin Genma Taisen* (artist: [Shotaro Ishinomori](/wiki/Shotaro_Ishinomori "Shotaro Ishinomori"))
* *Crystal Child* (artist: Ayumi Izumitani)
* *Bachigami* (artist: Yuuki Yogo)

* *Genma Taisen Rebirth* (Kyôichi Nanatsuki, artist: Masato Hayase, [Ishimori Productions](/wiki/Ishimori_Productions "Ishimori Productions"))
* *8 Man vs Cyborg 009* (Kyôichi Nanatsuki, artist: Masato Hayase, Ishimori Productions)
